name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 63 Thursday. 8-DAY KINETIC PAUSE CONTINUES; VLCC TD3C ~$423K/DAY LATE-MAR PEAK MODERATING; FRONTLINE $36.28 (+0.5% 1D, +8.7% 1M, +31.0% 3M); BOAT $40.70 (+0.3% 1D, +3.0% 1M, +14.9% 3M). Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8% Apr 29, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED, highest since June 2022) on Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First. Persian Gulf 'all but closed to shipping' per Lloyd's List. War-risk premium ~1% AWRP (1.5%-3% Strait transit, 5% for US/UK/Israeli vessels per Lloyd's List/Strauss Center; up to $7.5M-$14M per voyage) for negotiated transits — Lloyd's not stepping down on rhetoric. Per-VLCC incremental cost $6-10M/transit (hormuztoll.com). 80% drop in Hormuz transits sustained. Brent-WTI spread NARROWED $15→$13 — first session WTI catches Brent (US export-arbitrage compression; structural-resolution channel via record >6M bpd US exports per EIA, NOT Hormuz-resolution). Dated-vs-June Brent backwardation $20-25/bbl modestly compressed but still extreme. UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW May 1 — structural Gulf realignment but not directly tanker-relevant near-term. Houthis Day 67-68 quiet. CENTCOM 31-vessel blockade running rate ~1.3/day. Trump April 23 destroy-mine-laying-boats ROE in effect; no boats destroyed. CENTCOM 'short and powerful' strike plan PREPARED-not-activated. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): No commercial-vessel interdiction confirmed since Wed Apr 22 MSC Francesca/Epaminondas seizure — 7-day kinetic pause is longest non-IED IRGC operational quiet of blockade. April 23 ISDB transit dark-only (5 vessels all Northern Corridor) holds. Persian Gulf 'all but closed to shipping' per Lloyd's List. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
